## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On December 13, 1937, the incredibly massive, horrifically brutal, and historically devastating Nanking Massacre (Rape of Nanking) aggressively commenced in China. Following the massive capture of the Chinese capital by the Imperial Japanese Army, heavily armed Japanese soldiers unleashed an absolutely massive, six-week reign of unspeakable terror. The incredibly horrific, massive atrocities resulted in the brutal mass rape and tragic deaths of an estimated 200,000 to 300,000 Chinese civilians and disarmed soldiers, deeply scarring Sino-Japanese relations for centuries.

On December 13, 2003 (Historical context note), Saddam Hussein, the incredibly massive, deeply feared, and absolutely brutal former dictator of Iraq, was successfully and aggressively captured by United States military forces. Heavily hiding in an incredibly massive, tiny subterranean "spider hole" near his hometown of Tikrit, his massive, deeply humiliating capture heavily marked the absolute, final operational climax of Operation Red Dawn. His incredibly massive, globally televised apprehension completely shattered the absolute remnants of the Ba'athist regime's massive psychological grip on the nation.

On December 13, 2001 (Historical context note), an incredibly massive, completely unprecedented, and absolutely horrific terrorist attack violently struck the absolute heart of the Indian democratic republic.
